channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: AWS, 아마존 베드록 국내 정식 출시 “서울 리전에서 지원”
item.description: 아마존웹서비스(이하 AWS)가 3일 AWS 아시아 태평양(서울) 리전의 아마존 베드록(Amazon Bedrock)을 정식 출시한다고 발표했다. 아마존 베드록은 생성형 인공지능(AI) 애플리케이션을 구축하고 확장하려는 사용자가 특정 용도에 적합한 고성능 파운데이션 모델(FM) 및 거대언어모델(LLM)을 쉽게 찾고 접근할 수 있도록 하는 서비스다. AWS는 2023년부터 일부 지역을 시작으로 해당 서비스를 제공해오고 있다.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple MacBook Air M4: news, rumors, and everything we know
item.description: As rumors of an Apple October event swirl, hopes for a MacBook Air M4 refresh have started ramping up.
item.dc:creator: allisa.james@futurenet.com (Allisa James), TechRadar

item.title: NIST, Cryptography and The Future of VPNs
item.description: Anticipating this challenge, the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) has recently finalized a set of quantum-resistant encryption standards.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Toyota invests another $500 million in an electric air taxi company as it soars toward the future of air mobility
item.description: Toyota has injected a second round of funding into Joby Aviation, one of the leading figures in electric vertical take-off and landing craft for public transport.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Micron launches 36GB HBM3E memory as it plays catch up with Samsung and SK Hynix as archrivals frantically rush towards the next big thing — HBM4 with its 16 layers, 1.65TBps bandwidth, and 48GB SKUs
item.description: Micron's 36GB HBM3E 12-high memory offers 50% more capacity, 1.2TB/s bandwidth, and 30% lower power than competitors, fueling AI innovation.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Things get nasty in lawsuit between WordPress.org and WP Engine
item.description: After years of nasty back and forth accusations between WordPress hosting provider WP Engine (WPE) and WordPress.org, the open source project that provides the software, on Wednesday WPE sued WordPress.org in federal court, accusing WordPress.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Amazon faces NLRB complaint over refusing to bargain with drivers’ union
item.description: Amazon.com has been accused by a U.S. labor board of illegally refusing to bargain with a union representing drivers employed by a contractor, the agency announced on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: NYT Connections today — hints and answers for Friday, October 4 (game #481)
item.description: Looking for NYT Connections answers and hints? Here's all you need to know to solve today's game, plus my commentary on the puzzles.
item.dc:creator: marc.mclaren@futurenet.com (Marc McLaren), TechRadar

item.title: Google's search engine's latest AI injection will answer voiced questions about video and photos
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google is injecting its search engine with more artificial intelligence that will enable people to voice questions about images and occasionally organize an entire page of results, despite the technology's past misadventures with misleading information.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Elon Musk’s friendship with Diddy examined after he posted about boy’s alleged abuse
item.description: Sean 'Diddy' Combs' mentee Aubrey O'Day and others called out Musk for trying to distance himself from allegations that the music mogul sexually abused multiple victims, including a 9-year-old boy.
item.dc:creator: Martha Ross, The Mercury News

item.title: The bigger the better? Approaching Generative AI by size
item.description: As the Generative AI (GenAI) hype continues, we’re seeing an uptick of real-world, enterprise-grade solutions in industries from healthcare and finance, to retail and media.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The EU wants to know more about social media algorithms
item.description: Via the Digital Services Regulation (DSA), the European Commission has requested information from Youtube, Snapchat and Tiktok about which parameters their algorithms use to recommend social media content to users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Massachusetts struck a deal with Uber and Lyft for minimum pay. Drivers say they’re still getting shortchanged
item.description: Michael Moya started driving for Uber and Lyft in 2019, back when he was living in Florida. He liked it for a while. It provided flexible hours, which was a major advantage for the father of three. And the money was pretty good: He could easily make $300 in a six-hour shift.
item.dc:creator: Bryce Covert, Fast Company

item.title: What’s new for Apple Intelligence?
item.description: Most Apple watchers may have noticed that the company’s iPhone 16 marketing really does put Apple Intelligence front and center, even though its home-baked breed of AaI (Artificial [Apple] Intelligence) isn’t available quite yet.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: 10 ways AI can make your programming job easier
item.description: It is not surprising that programmers have been the primary beneficiaries of AI; after all, programming is precisely the sort of thing an AI can analyze. It’s a logical, structured science that relies on rules, conditions and structure.
item.dc:creator: Richard Baguley, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI continues to burn money
item.description: Although OpenAI’s revenues are increasing significantly, the generative AI (genAI) pioneer remains dependent on financial injections, according to Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The US and Microsoft disrupt a Russian hacking group targeting American officials and nonprofits
item.description: U.S. authorities and Microsoft say hackers linked to Russian intelligence targeted dozens of former military and intelligence officials, journalists and civil society groups with a sophisticated campaign designed to steal information.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Federal judge blocks new California law cracking down on election deepfakes
item.description: U.S. District Judge John A. Mendez said artificial intelligence and deepfakes pose significant risks, but he ruled that the law likely violates the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: OpenAI demands investors shun rivals such as Anthropic, Elon Musk’s xAI
item.description: OpenAI has raised $6.6 billion from investors like Thrive Capital and Tiger Global, but the AI company also sought assurances that investors would avoid funding five competing firms, according to a Reuters report.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tres cosas que hay que hacer bien en la gestión de datos para proyectos de IA generativa
item.description: Según Kari Briski, vicepresidente de Modelos, Software y Servicios de IA en Nvidia, implementar con éxito la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa depende de la gestión eficaz de los datos y de evaluar cómo los diferentes modelos trabajan juntos para servir a un caso de uso específico.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI on the mainframe? IBM may be onto something
item.description: CIOs have been moving workloads from legacy platforms to the cloud for more than a decade but the rush to AI may breathe new life into an old enterprise friend: the mainframe. At least IBM believes so.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Let your iPhone read aloud what you can't see
item.description: The new Reader Mode in iOS 18’s Magnifier app is here to help iPhone users read something small or in poor lighting. All you have to do is snap a picture.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google, it’s time to kill CAPTCHAS
item.description: Are you a robot? Google really, really wants to know. The answer to this question is demanded of web users 200 million times a day via CAPTCHAs — “Completely Automated Public Turing test to tell Computers and Humans Apart,” a system owned and operated by Google.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Windows 11: A guide to the updates
item.description: A Windows launch isn’t the end a process — it’s really just the beginning. Microsoft continually works on improving Windows 11 by fixing bugs, releasing security patches, and occasionally adding new features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Uber and Lyft are funding opposition efforts over San Francisco transit ballot
item.description: Uber and Lyft are funding opposition efforts over a ballot initiative in San Francisco that’s meant to increase funding for the city’s public transit system.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Spider lovers scurry to Colorado town in search of mating tarantulas and community
item.description: It’s tarantula mating season on the Colorado plains, when male spiders scurry out of their burrows in search of a mate.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Peipert And Hannah Schoenbaum, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane Helene brings climate change to forefront of the presidential campaign
item.description: The devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene has brought climate change to the forefront of the presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Daly, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: California spiked a landmark AI regulation. But that doesn’t mean the bill is going away
item.description: With the veto of California’s AI bill, the idea of regulating frontier models may be in jeopardy. The bill, SB 1047, would have required developers of the largest AI models (OpenAI, Anthropic, and the like) to set up and report on a safety framework, and submit to outside safety audits.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Bumble, Grindr, and Match have agreed to Australia’s online dating code of conduct
item.description: A code of conduct will be enforced on the online dating industry to better protect Australian users after research found that three-in-four people suffer some form of sexual violence through the platforms, Australia’s government said on Tuesday. Bumble, Grindr and Match Group Inc.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI previews Realtime API for speech-to-speech apps
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a public beta of the Realtime API, an API that allows paid developers to build low-latency, multi-modal experiences including text and speech in apps.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta and Australia have partnered to crack down on ‘celeb bait’ scam ads
item.description: Meta said it has taken down some 8,000 so-called “celeb bait” scam ads from Facebook and Instagram as part of a new effort with Australian banks to curb the practice.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Biden signs bill cutting environmental reviews for semiconductor industry
item.description: President Biden on Wednesday signed legislation exempting semiconductor manufacturing facilities from environmental reviews — despite objections from some Democrats and green activists who have expressed concerns about the potential for toxic waste.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, The Hill

item.title: Snapchat’s failure to protect kids revealed in New Mexico lawsuit
item.description: Snapchat failed to act on “rampant” reports of child grooming, sextortion and other dangers to minors on its platform, according to a newly unredacted complaint against the company filed by New Mexico’s attorney general. Attorney General Raúl Torrez filed the original complaint on Sept.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Apple accused of violating labor laws, again
item.description: Apple has been accused of violating union rights, according to a complaint filed by the US National Labor Relations Board (NLRB) .
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Women Congressional Candidates Face Increased Hate Speech on Social Platform X - Study Reveals Alarming Trends
item.description: A new study by the Center for Democracy and Technology and the University of Pittsburgh reveals that women congressional candidates, particularly those of color, are targeted with hate speech at alarming rates on social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: San Jose Fry’s Electronics site is poised to become huge tech hub
item.description: Super Micro Computer is floating a plan at San Jose City Hall to develop a brand-new building totaling 333,400 square feet that would be part of a tech hub in north San Jose.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information
item.description: The IG said CISA made some good improvements to the Automated Indicator Sharing program in recent years. The post CISA faces challenges to sharing cyber threat information first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: IBM Unveils First Quantum Data Center Outside the US, Boosting European Innovation
item.description: IBM's latest quantum data center in Ehningen, Germany aims to advance quantum computing and talent development in Europe
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Tesla posts first quarterly increase in deliveries, but shares slump with investors hoping for more
item.description: Low interest financing, sweet lease deals, price cuts and free charging boosted Tesla’s global deliveries in the third quarter, the first increase this year for the electric vehicle maker.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11
